title: Learning from Home: The New Way to Study
passage:
Learning from Home: The New Way to Study

Online learning is very popular today. Many students do not go to a traditional school building. Instead, they study at home using a computer and the internet. This is also called distance education.

To study online, students need three important things. First, they need a computer or a laptop. Second, they need a fast internet connection. Third, they need a quiet room to study without noise. Every morning, students open their computers and log in to their school website. On this website, they can see their lessons, watch videos, and do their homework. They can also send messages to their teachers and classmates.

Some online classes are live. This means the teacher talks and the students listen at the same time. Other classes are recorded. This means the teacher makes a video before the class, and students can watch the video at any time they want.

Online learning has many benefits. First, students can save time and money. They do not need to travel to school by bus, train, or car. Second, students can study at their own speed. If a lesson is difficult, they can watch the video again. Third, students can learn from any place. They can study at home, in a library, or even at a quiet cafe.

However, online learning also has some problems. Sometimes, the internet connection is slow, and the video stops. Also, some students feel lonely because they do not see their friends in person. They cannot play games together during the school break.

To study well online, students must have a good daily plan. They should wake up early, wear clean clothes, and sit at a study desk. They must also turn off their mobile phones to focus on the lesson. Online learning is not always easy, but it helps many people around the world.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do students use to study online?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 says: 'Instead, they study at home using a computer and the internet.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

options.B: The teacher talks and students listen at the same time.
options.C: Students watch videos from last year.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 says: 'Some online classes are live. This means the teacher talks and the students listen at the same time.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why can students save time with online learning?

options.B: They do not need to travel to school.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 says: 'First, students can save time and money. They do not need to travel to school by bus, train, or car.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why do some students feel lonely?

options.B: They do not see their friends in person.
options.C: They do not like their teachers.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 says: 'Also, some students feel lonely because they do not see their friends in person.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Students need a quiet room to study online.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Third, they need a quiet room to study without noise.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Recorded classes mean students must watch videos at exactly 8:00 AM.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Other classes are recorded... students can watch the video at any time they want.' This contradicts the idea that they must watch it at exactly 8:00 AM.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Most online students live in England.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: The text does not mention England or where most online students live.

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: To study online, students must log in to their school ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 says: 'Every morning, students open their computers and log in to their school website.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: If a lesson is difficult, students can watch the ______ again.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 says: 'If a lesson is difficult, they can watch the video again.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Students must turn off their ______ to focus on the lesson.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 6 says: 'They must also turn off their mobile phones to focus on the lesson.'

vocabulary.meaning: following ideas or methods that have existed for a long time
a system of education where students study at home using the internet
sad because you are alone or have no friends near you
to give all your attention to something

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Kỹ thuật tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ này, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ vựng giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy dùng bút chì gạch chân từ khóa trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'log in to', 'difficult', 'lonely') rồi tìm đúng từ đó trong bài đọc để tìm ra đáp án nhanh nhất.

2. True/False/Not Given: Đối với câu 'Not Given', nếu bạn đọc hết bài mà hoàn toàn không tìm thấy thông tin về tên quốc gia (như 'England') hay số liệu cụ thể, hãy chọn ngay NOT GIVEN.

3. Điền từ vào chỗ trống: Chú ý giới hạn số từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Từ cần điền phải được chép chính xác từ bài đọc, không thay đổi dạng của từ (không thêm 's' hoặc 'ed' nếu trong bài không có).
